Examples.Title: Generate vectors
Examples.Description: Generate vector embeddings for input data. This endpoint uses Pinecone's [hosted embedding models](https://docs.pinecone.io/guides/index-data/create-an-index#embedding-models).
Examples.Code:
### Generate vectors
# @name embed
POST {{host}}/embed
Api-Key: {{api_key}}
X-Pinecone-Api-Version: 2026-04
Content-Type: application/json
Accept: application/json

{
  "model": "multilingual-e5-large",
  "parameters": {
    "input_type": "passage",
    "truncate": "END"
  },
  "inputs": [
    {
      "text": "The quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog."
    }
  ]
}

## Responses
# 200
# Description: OK
# Content-Type: application/json
# 400
# Description: Bad request. The request body included invalid request parameters.
# Content-Type: application/json
# 401
# Description: Unauthorized. Possible causes: Invalid API key.
# Content-Type: application/json
# 500
# Description: Internal server error.
# Content-Type: application/json

Examples.Title: Describe a model
Examples.Description:
Get a description of a model hosted by Pinecone. 

You can use hosted models as an integrated part of Pinecone operations or for standalone embedding and reranking. For more details, see [Vector embedding](https://docs.pinecone.io/guides/index-data/indexing-overview#vector-embedding) and [Rerank results](https://docs.pinecone.io/guides/search/rerank-results).
Examples.Setup: This example assumes `using Pinecone;` is in scope and `apiKey` contains the required credential.

Examples.Title: List available models
Examples.Description:
List the embedding and reranking models hosted by Pinecone. 

You can use hosted models as an integrated part of Pinecone operations or for standalone embedding and reranking. For more details, see [Vector embedding](https://docs.pinecone.io/guides/index-data/indexing-overview#vector-embedding) and [Rerank results](https://docs.pinecone.io/guides/search/rerank-results).
Examples.Setup: This example assumes `using Pinecone;` is in scope and `apiKey` contains the required credential.

Examples.Title: Rerank results
Examples.Description:
Rerank results according to their relevance to a query.

For guidance and examples, see [Rerank results](https://docs.pinecone.io/guides/search/rerank-results).
Examples.Code:
### Rerank results
# @name rerank
POST {{host}}/rerank
Api-Key: {{api_key}}
X-Pinecone-Api-Version: 2026-04
Content-Type: application/json
Accept: application/json

{
  "model": "bge-reranker-v2-m3",
  "query": "What is the capital of France?",
  "top_n": 5,
  "return_documents": true,
  "rank_fields": [
    "text"
  ],
  "documents": [
    {
      "id": "1",
      "text": "Paris is the capital of France.",
      "title": "France",
      "url": "https://example.com"
    }
  ],
  "parameters": {
    "truncate": "END"
  }
}

## Responses
# 200
# Description: OK
# Content-Type: application/json
# 400
# Description: Bad request. The request body included invalid request parameters.
# Content-Type: application/json
# 401
# Description: Unauthorized. Possible causes: Invalid API key.
# Content-Type: application/json
# 500
# Description: Internal server error.
# Content-Type: application/json

Examples.Title: Configure an index
Examples.Description: Configure an existing index. For guidance and examples, see [Manage indexes](https://docs.pinecone.io/guides/manage-data/manage-indexes).
Examples.Setup: This example assumes `using Pinecone;` is in scope and `apiKey` contains the required credential.

Examples.Title: Create a backup of an index
Examples.Description: Create a backup of an index.
Examples.Code:
### Create a backup of an index
# @name create_backup
POST {{host}}/indexes/{{index_name}}/backups
Api-Key: {{api_key}}
X-Pinecone-Api-Version: 2026-04
Content-Type: application/json
Accept: application/json

{}

## Responses
# 201
# Description: The backup has been successfully created.
# Content-Type: application/json
# 400
# Description: Bad request. The request body included invalid request parameters.
# Content-Type: application/json
# 401
# Description: Unauthorized. Possible causes: Invalid API key.
# Content-Type: application/json
# 402
# Description: Payment required. Organization is on a paid plan and is delinquent on payment.
# Content-Type: application/json
# 403
# Description: You've exceed your backup quota.
# Content-Type: application/json
# 422
# Description: Unprocessable entity. The request body could not be deserialized.
# Content-Type: application/json
# 500
# Description: Internal server error.
# Content-Type: application/json

Examples.Title: Create a collection
Examples.Description:
Create a Pinecone collection.
  
Serverless indexes do not support collections.
Examples.Setup: This example assumes `using Pinecone;` is in scope and `apiKey` contains the required credential.

Examples.Title: Create an index
Examples.Description:
Create a Pinecone index. This is where you specify the measure of similarity, the dimension of vectors to be stored in the index, which cloud provider you would like to deploy with, and more.
  
For guidance and examples, see [Create an index](https://docs.pinecone.io/guides/index-data/create-an-index).
Examples.Setup: This example assumes `using Pinecone;` is in scope and `apiKey` contains the required credential.

Examples.Title: Create an index with integrated embedding
Examples.Description:
Create an index with integrated embedding.
With this type of index, you provide source text, and  Pinecone uses a [hosted embedding model](https://docs.pinecone.io/guides/index-data/create-an-index#embedding-models)  to convert the text automatically during [upsert](https://docs.pinecone.io/reference/api/2026-04/data-plane/upsert_records)  and [search](https://docs.pinecone.io/reference/api/2026-04/data-plane/search_records).  
For guidance and examples, see [Create an index](https://docs.pinecone.io/guides/index-data/create-an-index#integrated-embedding).
Examples.Setup: This example assumes `using Pinecone;` is in scope and `apiKey` contains the required credential.

Examples.Title: Create an index from a backup
Examples.Description: Create an index from a backup.
Examples.Code:
### Create an index from a backup
# @name create_index_from_backup_operation
POST {{host}}/backups/{{backup_id}}/create-index
Api-Key: {{api_key}}
X-Pinecone-Api-Version: 2026-04
Content-Type: application/json
Accept: application/json

{
  "name": "example-index",
  "tags": {
    "tag0": "val0",
    "tag1": "val1"
  },
  "deletion_protection": "disabled"
}

## Responses
# 202
# Description: The request to create the index has been accepted.
# Content-Type: application/json
# 400
# Description: Bad request. The request body included invalid request parameters.
# Content-Type: application/json
# 401
# Description: Unauthorized. Possible causes: Invalid API key.
# Content-Type: application/json
# 402
# Description: Payment required. Organization is on a paid plan and is delinquent on payment.
# Content-Type: application/json
# 403
# Description: You've exceed your pod quota.
# Content-Type: application/json
# 404
# Description: Backup not found.
# Content-Type: application/json
# 409
# Description: Index of given name already exists.
# Content-Type: application/json
# 422
# Description: Unprocessable entity. The request body could not be deserialized.
# Content-Type: application/json
# 500
# Description: Internal server error.
# Content-Type: application/json

Examples.Title: List backups for an index
Examples.Description: List all backups for an index.
Examples.Code:
### List backups for an index
# @name list_index_backups
GET {{host}}/indexes/{{index_name}}/backups?limit=10&paginationToken={{paginationToken}}
Api-Key: {{api_key}}
X-Pinecone-Api-Version: 2026-04
Accept: application/json

## Responses
# 200
# Description: This operation returns a list of all the backups that you have previously created, and which are associated with the given index.
# Content-Type: application/json
# 401
# Description: Unauthorized. Possible causes: Invalid API key.
# Content-Type: application/json
# 404
# Description: Index not found.
# Content-Type: application/json
# 500
# Description: Internal server error.
# Content-Type: application/json
